---
title: ""
---

:::{.hero-section}
# First Synthesis

:::{.lead}
First Synthesis LLC engineers computational foundations for formal verification and neuro-symbolic AI. The flagship product, the **Monist Engine**, abandons traditional hierarchical type-checking. By utilizing geometric shortest-path routing, the engine natively compiles structurally dense paradoxes into lock-free GPU Interaction Nets. Built for absolute structural certainty.
:::

:::{.action-buttons}
<a href="https://github.com/ryanncode/monist" class="btn-primary">View GitHub</a>
<a href="whitepaper.qmd" class="btn-secondary">Read Whitepaper</a>
:::
:::

## Autonomous GPU Dispatch

*Monist executes highly parallel combinatorial reductions directly in VRAM, completely bypassing CPU synchronization overhead.*

```{=html}
<div class="terminal-window">
  <div class="terminal-header">
    <div class="dots">
      <div class="dot"></div>
      <div class="dot"></div>
      <div class="dot"></div>
    </div>
    <span>bash — monist-core/kosaraju-scc</span>
  </div>
  <div class="terminal-body">
    <div class="terminal-placeholder">
      [ Placeholder: Terminal Visual Logic Operation GIF 1 ]<br><br>
      > Flattening 0-weight semantic cycles...<br>
      > Measuring thermodynamic weights...
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="terminal-window">
  <div class="terminal-header">
    <div class="dots">
      <div class="dot"></div>
      <div class="dot"></div>
      <div class="dot"></div>
    </div>
    <span>bash — monist-comb/reduce.wgsl</span>
  </div>
  <div class="terminal-body">
    <div class="terminal-placeholder">
      [ Placeholder: Terminal Visual Logic Operation GIF 2 ]<br><br>
      > Allocating 32-bit Pointers in GPU Arena...<br>
      > Lock-Free CAS Parallel Graph Rewire Active...
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Architecture Features

:::{#feature-grid-view}
::::{.feature-grid}

:::{.feature-card .interactive-card data-target="detail-itp"}
### Hybrid ITP Interface
Fuses human-guided natural deduction with an automated, bare-metal graph reduction engine. Users can actively steer high-level proofs while the system seamlessly handles raw topological complexity.
:::

:::{.feature-card .interactive-card data-target="detail-oracle"}
### The Oracle Layer
Transforms standard first-order logic into algebraic constraint matrices. Monist synthesizes dynamic T-Functors to absorb structural friction during dense recursion.
:::

:::{.feature-card .interactive-card data-target="detail-wgpu"}
### WGPU Physics Backend
32-Bit Tagged Pointers in a native Arena. Continuous evaluation until logical fixpoint via WGSL compute shaders, bypassing 64-bit atomic limits.
:::

:::{.feature-card .interactive-card data-target="detail-formal"}
### Formal Verification
Continuously compiles structural topologies into SMT-LIB witnesses directly into Lean for parallel proof-theoretic validation.
:::

::::
:::

:::{#feature-detail-view style="display: none; padding: 2rem; background: #fafafa; border: 1px solid #eaeaea; border-radius: 8px; margin-top: 2rem;"}
<button id="return-to-grid" class="btn-secondary" style="margin-bottom: 2rem; cursor: pointer; border: none;">&laquo; Return to Features</button>

<div id="detail-content-container"></div>
:::

:::{#detail-itp style="display: none;"}
### Hybrid ITP Interface: Deep Dive

The Hybrid Interactive Theorem Prover (ITP) interface merges the intuitive, goal-directed exploration of traditional proof assistants with the raw, mechanical speed of our graph reduction engine. 

- **Human-in-the-Loop:** Direct the high-level strategy while the engine automates the tedious combinatorics.
- **Topological Navigation:** Visualize proofs not as text, but as spatial graph structures.
- **Instant Feedback:** As you build propositions, the backend immediately begins reducing sub-graphs to normal form in the background.

```{=html}
<div class="terminal-window" style="margin-top: 2rem;">
  <div class="terminal-header">
    <div class="dots"><div class="dot"></div><div class="dot"></div><div class="dot"></div></div>
    <span>ITP Interface Preview</span>
  </div>
  <div class="terminal-body"><div class="terminal-placeholder">
    [ Placeholder: ITP Interface Video/GIF ]
  </div></div>
</div>
```
:::

:::{#detail-oracle style="display: none;"}
### The Oracle Layer: Deep Dive

Standard first-order logic struggles with paradox and structural friction. The Oracle Layer acts as a mathematical shock-absorber.

- **Algebraic Constraint Matrices:** We compile propositions into spatial matrices rather than linear ASTs.
- **Dynamic T-Functors:** When infinite recursion or "liar paradoxes" are detected, the Oracle automatically synthesizes T-Functors to quarantine the cycle without halting the entire system.
- **Holographic Phase Space:** Embeds the discrete graph into a continuous vector space for heuristic guidance.

```{=html}
<div class="terminal-window" style="margin-top: 2rem;">
  <div class="terminal-header">
    <div class="dots"><div class="dot"></div><div class="dot"></div><div class="dot"></div></div>
    <span>Oracle Layer Visualization</span>
  </div>
  <div class="terminal-body"><div class="terminal-placeholder">
    [ Placeholder: Oracle Layer Visualization GIF ]
  </div></div>
</div>
```
:::

:::{#detail-wgpu style="display: none;"}
### WGPU Physics Backend: Deep Dive

This is where the Monist Engine abandons traditional CPU architectures. We treat computation as a physical process occurring in VRAM.

- **Native Arena:** 32-Bit Tagged Pointers eliminate the overhead of 64-bit memory addressing, allowing models to fit entirely within high-speed GPU cache.
- **Lock-Free CAS:** Compare-And-Swap algorithms enable thousands of parallel threads to rewrite the graph simultaneously without blocking.
- **WGSL Compute Shaders:** WebGPU shaders ensure cross-platform bare-metal performance, from standard laptops to enterprise AI clusters.

```{=html}
<div class="terminal-window" style="margin-top: 2rem;">
  <div class="terminal-header">
    <div class="dots"><div class="dot"></div><div class="dot"></div><div class="dot"></div></div>
    <span>WGPU Arena Profiler</span>
  </div>
  <div class="terminal-body"><div class="terminal-placeholder">
    [ Placeholder: WGPU Arena Profiler GIF ]
  </div></div>
</div>
```
:::

:::{#detail-formal style="display: none;"}
### Formal Verification: Deep Dive

Absolute structural certainty requires mathematical rigor. While the engine runs extremely fast, it also continuously proves its own work.

- **SMT-LIB Witnesses:** As the graph reduces, it emits standard SMT-LIB witnesses.
- **Lean Integration:** These witnesses are piped directly into the Lean 4 Theorem Prover.
- **Parallel Validation:** Lean runs on a separate CPU thread pool, proving the topological reductions are sound without slowing down the GPU physics engine.

```{=html}
<div class="terminal-window" style="margin-top: 2rem;">
  <div class="terminal-header">
    <div class="dots"><div class="dot"></div><div class="dot"></div><div class="dot"></div></div>
    <span>Lean Verification Output</span>
  </div>
  <div class="terminal-body"><div class="terminal-placeholder">
    [ Placeholder: Lean Verification Output GIF ]
  </div></div>
</div>
```
:::

```{=html}
<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
    const gridView = document.getElementById('feature-grid-view');
    const detailView = document.getElementById('feature-detail-view');
    const detailContentContainer = document.getElementById('detail-content-container');
    const returnBtn = document.getElementById('return-to-grid');
    const cards = document.querySelectorAll('.interactive-card');

    cards.forEach(card => {
        // Add hover styles
        card.style.cursor = 'pointer';
        card.style.transition = 'transform 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease';
        card.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => {
            card.style.transform = 'translateY(-5px)';
            card.style.borderColor = 'var(--primary-color)';
        });
        card.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
            card.style.transform = 'translateY(0)';
            card.style.borderColor = 'rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1)';
        });

        // Click handler
        card.addEventListener('click', () => {
            const targetId = card.getAttribute('data-target');
            const targetElement = document.getElementById(targetId);
            
            // Clone the content so we don't destroy the original
            detailContentContainer.innerHTML = targetElement.innerHTML;
            
            // Swap views
            gridView.style.display = 'none';
            detailView.style.display = 'block';
            
            // Optional: smooth scroll
            detailView.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
        });
    });

    returnBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
        detailView.style.display = 'none';
        gridView.style.display = 'block';
        // Scroll back to the grid view
        gridView.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
    });
});
</script>
```
